title: Episodic recall: 'what did we decide about X last month' answered from conversation history
current_state: Past conversations exist as L1 rows (30d) and turns.jsonl, but no retrieval path treats a past conversation as a recallable episode — recall questions hit distilled L2/L3 facts only, losing the narrative ('we discussed three options and picked B because...').
target_state: recall_episode ToolSpec: query → top-k past conversation spans (from the T-186 store + L1) via hybrid search, returned as compact episode digests (when, mode, what was discussed, what was decided) the model can quote. Prefetch (_prefetch_memory) optionally taps it for 'remember when / what did we decide / last time' phrasings.
migration_plan: 1. Episode index: per-conversation digest row (title + summary + entities) maintained at conversation close (Groq tier='cheap'), stored in the conversations table.
2. recall_episode: hybrid search over digests, then optional drill-down into stored turns for the top hit.
3. Prefetch trigger phrases ('what did we decide', 'last time', 'remember when') route to episode search before fact search.
4. Tests: seeded store → decision question retrieves the right episode; digest written at close; drill-down returns real turns.
risk_notes: Token discipline: episode digests in prompt capped like prefetch (~4 hits, 200 chars). Build AFTER T-186's store exists — do not bolt onto raw turns.jsonl.
